name: Puente de Valdelasmozas from El Campillo – circular via Almendra and Andavías
name_original: Puente de Valdelasmozas desde El Campillo – circular por Almendra y Andavías
region_intro: Hiking around San Pedro De La Nave-Almendra offers diverse landscapes, characterized by the dramatic river canyons of the Arribes del Duero Natural Park and the deep geographical depression of the Arribes del Esla. The region features significant natural landmarks such as the Embalse de Ricobayo reservoir and various waterfalls, including Cascada de La Palia and Cadozos Waterfall. These natural features provide a rich backdrop for exploring the area's unique terrain, which includes impressive stone formations and valleys along the Esla River. Best hiking trails around San Pedro De La Nave-Almendra The most popular hiking route is La Palia Waterfall - round trip from Valdoradas bridge, a 2.1 miles (3.4 km) trail that takes 57 minutes to complete. This easy path leads to a well-known waterfall, particularly impressive after heavy rains. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Perones and Cadozos waterfall — circular from Ricobayo de Alba, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.8 km) path. This route explores the area around the Ricobayo Reservoir and the Cadozos Waterfall. Local hikers also love the Puente de Valdelasmozas from El Campillo – circular via Almendra and Andavías, a 15.7 miles (25.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 6 hours 44 minutes. Hiking around San Pedro De La Nave-Almendra is defined by dramatic river canyons, reservoirs, and seasonal waterfalls. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in San Pedro De La Nave-Almendra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. More than 18 hikers have used komoot to explore San Pedro De La Nave-Almendra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in San Pedro De La Nave-Almendra?
A: The region offers a selection of 4 hiking routes, catering to various preferences and skill levels. These include 1 easy, 2 moderate, and 1 difficult trail.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the area is known for its waterfalls. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026734793/la-palia-waterfall-round-trip-from-valdoradas-bridge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Palia Waterfall - round trip from Valdoradas bridge&lt;/a&gt;, which is particularly impressive after heavy rains. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026677750/route-of-the-perones-and-cadozos-waterfall-circular-from-ricobayo-de-alba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Perones and Cadozos waterfall — circular from Ricobayo de Alba&lt;/a&gt;, which leads to the Cadozos Waterfall.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Hiking in San Pedro De La Nave-Almendra offers dramatic river canyons, impressive stone formations, and valleys along the Esla River. The region is characterized by the deep geographical depression of the Arribes del Esla and is close to the Arribes del Duero Natural Park, providing spectacular views of the Duero River.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026677750/route-of-the-perones-and-cadozos-waterfall-circular-from-ricobayo-de-alba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Perones and Cadozos waterfall — circular from Ricobayo de Alba&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 4.8-mile path. For a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1123665143/puente-de-valdelasmozas-from-el-campillo-circular-via-almendra-and-andavias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Valdelasmozas from El Campillo – circular via Almendra and Andavías&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 15.7-mile trail.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to see while hiking?
A: The area is rich in both natural and historical sights. You can visit the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5714203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro de la Nave&lt;/a&gt;, a Visigothic church moved stone by stone to its current location. Other points of interest include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdoradas Bridge&lt;/a&gt; also serves as a starting point for several routes and offers great views.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options?
A: Yes, for an easy and family-friendly option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026734793/la-palia-waterfall-round-trip-from-valdoradas-bridge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Palia Waterfall - round trip from Valdoradas bridge&lt;/a&gt; is a 2.1-mile trail that is generally considered easy and takes less than an hour to complete, making it suitable for most ages.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in this region?
A: The region's waterfalls, such as Cascada de La Palia and Cadozos Waterfall, are most spectacular after heavy rains, typically in late autumn, winter, or early spring. For general hiking, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, while summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in San Pedro De La Nave-Almendra?
A: The trails in San Pedro De La Nave-Almendra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic river canyons, the impressive reservoirs, and the seasonal waterfalls that define the area's unique terrain.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: Many trails in natural areas like San Pedro De La Nave-Almendra are generally dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash. However, it's always recommended to check specific trail regulations or local signage for any restrictions, particularly in protected areas or near wildlife habitats.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting?
A: The proximity to the Arribes del Duero Natural Park, known for its rich biodiversity, suggests good opportunities for wildlife spotting. Hikers might encounter various bird species, including raptors, and other local fauna within the rugged river canyons and valleys.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1123665143/puente-de-valdelasmozas-from-el-campillo-circular-via-almendra-and-andavias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Valdelasmozas from El Campillo – circular via Almendra and Andavías&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 15.7-mile trail that typically takes around 6 hours and 44 minutes to complete, offering varied terrain and extensive exploration.
tips: The Church of San Pedro de la Nave is a 7th century Visigoth temple located in the town of El Campillo, municipality of San Pedro de la Nave-Almendra, in the province of Zamora, Spain. It was declared a National Monument on April 22, 1912. The construction of the temple dates back to the end of the 7th century and the beginning of the 8th century, possibly between the years 680 and 711, which makes it one of the last works of Visigothic art. Originally, it was located on the banks of the Esla River, but when the Ricobayo reservoir was built, it was moved stone by stone to the current location.
Bridge over the mouth of the reservoir, the Valdelasmozas Bridge has a firm narrow gravel.
Andavias is a small agricultural town with origins in the Middle Ages. There is a small stone bridge over the stream that crosses the town that leads to the San Miguel Arcángel Parish Church.

waypoints.name: Church of San Pedro de la Nave
San Miguel Arcángel Parish Church and Stone Bridge of Andavías
